title: Claude Corps Fellowship 2026–2027: Paid $85,000 AI Fellowship for Early-Career Professionals in the United States
summary: Applications Open for Anthropic s Claude Corps Fellowship Program Opportunity Summary Details Information Program Claude Corps Fellowship Organizer Anthropic Location United States Duration 12 Months Salary $85,000 per year Eligibility 18+ years old with less than 2 years of full-time work experience Application Deadline July 17, 2026 First Cohort Starts October 2026 Relocation Support Available Number of Fellows 1,000 (across cohorts) Artificial intelligence is rapidly transforming workplaces across the world, creating new opportunities while also reshaping traditional career paths. To help ensure that the benefits of AI are shared widely, Anthropic has launched Claude Corps , a groundbreaking national fellowship program that will train and place 1,000 early-career professionals in nonprofit organizations across the United States. Backed by an initial investment of $150 million , the program aims to equip nonprofits with AI expertise while providing fellows with valuable professional experience, hands-on training, and career development opportunities. What is Claude Corps? Claude Corps is a year-long paid fellowship designed to help nonprofits leverage the power of AI through Anthropic s Claude platform. The initiative brings together talented young professionals and mission-driven organizations to advance social impact while preparing the next generation of AI-literate workers. The program is a collaboration between: Anthropic – Funding the program, providing AI expertise, and leading overall strategy. CodePath – Serving as the employer of record and delivering fellowship programming and mentorship. Social Finance – Leading monitoring, evaluation, and long-term sustainability planning. The fellowship seeks to create a scalable model that demonstrates how AI can strengthen communities, organizations, and career pathways during a period of significant technological change. Benefits of the Claude Corps Fellowship Selected fellows will receive: Full-time salary of $85,000 per year Comprehensive benefits package Intensive AI training focused on nonprofit applications Ongoing professional development and mentorship Dedicated support from CodePath mentors Technical office hours with Anthropic experts Access to an extensive Claude AI token budget Professional supervision within host organizations Opportunity to build highly sought-after AI skills Fellowship Structure The fellowship lasts 12 months . At the beginning of the program, fellows will participate in intensive training focused on applying Claude and AI tools within nonprofit settings. Once placed with host organizations, fellows will continue receiving approximately five hours of weekly training , while spending the remainder of their time supporting their host organization s mission and operations. Host Organizations Over the next year, more than 400 nonprofit organizations are expected to host Claude Corps Fellows. Examples of participating organizations include: Braven Code the Dream Heartland Forward Montgomery County Food Bank Team Red, White Blue Reef Environmental Education Foundation SoundOff StriveTogether YMCA of Greater Charlotte These organizations work across sectors including education, workforce development, food security, veteran support, conservation, mental health, and community development. Who Can Apply? The Claude Corps Fellowship is open to individuals who: Are at least 18 years old Have less than two years of full-time work experience Are authorized to work in the United States Are comfortable working with AI tools such as Claude Are willing to relocate if necessary (relocation support is available) Importantly, applicants from all educational backgrounds are encouraged to apply. A college degree is not required. Fellowship Timeline First Cohort Application Deadline: July 17, 2026 Fellowship Start Date: October 2026 Number of Fellows: 100 Future Cohorts January 2027 August 2027 Applications for future cohorts are being accepted on a rolling basis. Why Claude Corps Matters As AI continues to transform industries, many experts have raised concerns about workforce disruption and unequal access to emerging technologies. Claude Corps represents a proactive effort to address these challenges by investing directly in people and organizations that can benefit from AI adoption. The initiative aims to: Expand AI literacy among early-career professionals Strengthen nonprofit effectiveness and impact Create new career pathways in the AI economy Generate evidence on how AI can support mission-driven work Develop a scalable model that can be replicated nationally and internationally Anthropic plans to rigorously evaluate the program s impact on both fellows and host organizations, with the goal of expanding the model beyond the initial 1,000 participants and potentially adapting it for use in other countries. How to Apply Applications for the Claude Corps Fellowship are now open. Interested candidates should prepare their applications before the July 17, 2026 deadline for the first cohort. This is a unique opportunity for aspiring professionals interested in artificial intelligence, social impact, nonprofit innovation, and community development to gain hands-on experience while earning a competitive salary and helping organizations advance their missions. Applications can be submitted here . The application asks for: Background and experience Short-answer questions, including one about a time the applicant made an impact in their community, and one about a time they learned from a mistake or setback Completion of two short AI training modules ( AI Fluency and Claude 101 ) hosted on Skilljar—this gives applicants a preview of the training fellows receive, and helps the program see how they prompt, evaluate an AI’s response, and adjust when the first answer is not quite right (instructions are included in the application) Demographic information (voluntary, used for program reporting and equity tracking) PROSPECTIVE FELLOWS SHOULD CLICK AND FIND MORE HERE PROSPECTIVE HOSTS CAN FIND MORE HERE Looking for more fully funded fellowships and IA related opportunities ? Click here https://opportunitiesforyouth.org/?s=AI The post Claude Corps Fellowship 2026–2027: Paid $85,000 AI Fellowship for Early-Career Professionals in the United States appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Marshall Memorial Fellowship 2027: Fully Funded Transatlantic Leadership Program for Emerging Leaders
summary: The Marshall Memorial Fellowship (MMF), the flagship leadership development initiative of the German Marshall Fund of the United States (GMF), is now preparing for its 2027 cohort. Since its launch in 1982, the fellowship has served as one of the most prestigious transatlantic leadership programs, connecting exceptional leaders from Europe and North America and equipping them with the knowledge, skills, networks, and experiences needed to address complex global challenges. Designed as a year-long leadership development experience, the Marshall Memorial Fellowship offers participants the opportunity to deepen their understanding of transatlantic relations, strengthen leadership competencies, engage with diverse communities, and build lasting professional relationships across borders. The program combines virtual engagement, immersive travel experiences, peer learning opportunities, networking events, and leadership development activities to cultivate a new generation of leaders capable of advancing democratic values, public diplomacy, and international cooperation. About the Marshall Memorial Fellowship The Marshall Memorial Fellowship was established by the German Marshall Fund of the United States in 1982. Originally created to introduce emerging European leaders to the United States, the program has evolved significantly over the decades. Today, MMF brings together leaders from both sides of the Atlantic, fostering meaningful exchanges and collaborative relationships that contribute to stronger transatlantic partnerships. The fellowship honors the spirit of international cooperation and mutual understanding that has long characterized transatlantic relations. Through extensive engagement with fellow participants, institutions, policymakers, and communities, fellows gain invaluable insights into contemporary social, political, economic, and cultural issues. The program continues to adapt to changing global realities while maintaining its core mission of strengthening leadership and international collaboration. Objectives of the Fellowship The Marshall Memorial Fellowship seeks to achieve several important goals, including: 1. Strengthening Transatlantic Leadership The fellowship prepares participants to become influential leaders capable of navigating increasingly complex international environments. 2. Building International Networks Participants develop meaningful relationships with peers, experts, and decision-makers from diverse sectors and countries. 3. Promoting Democratic Values The program supports leaders who are committed to democratic governance, civic engagement, inclusion, and public service. 4. Encouraging Cross-Cultural Understanding Fellows engage with diverse perspectives, helping them develop a broader understanding of global issues and solutions. 5. Enhancing Professional Skills Participants strengthen critical leadership competencies including: Strategic thinking Public engagement Collaborative leadership Policy analysis Cross-cultural communication Community-building International cooperation What Makes the Marshall Memorial Fellowship Unique? Unlike many traditional fellowship programs, MMF offers an intensive and comprehensive leadership journey that extends over an entire year. Participants benefit from: Structured leadership development activities Virtual learning sessions In-person meetings Three weeks of immersive international travel Peer-to-peer learning opportunities Exposure to diverse viewpoints Professional networking opportunities Access to a prestigious alumni network The fellowship emphasizes experiential learning, enabling participants to learn directly from communities, institutions, experts, and fellow leaders. Fellowship Structure The Marshall Memorial Fellowship is carefully designed to maximize learning and leadership growth. Key Components Include: Virtual Engagement Throughout the year, fellows participate in online sessions focused on leadership, policy issues, and transatlantic cooperation. In-Person Meetings Participants engage in face-to-face activities that strengthen relationships and deepen collaboration. Immersive Travel Experience One of the most distinctive features of the fellowship is approximately three weeks of immersive travel. During this period, fellows gain firsthand exposure to different political, economic, and social systems while interacting with influential leaders and organizations. Peer Learning The program creates opportunities for fellows to learn from each other s professional experiences, perspectives, and expertise. Networking Opportunities Participants establish relationships that often continue long after the fellowship concludes. Benefits of the Marshall Memorial Fellowship Selected fellows gain access to numerous benefits, including: Leadership Development Intensive leadership training Personal and professional growth opportunities Enhanced leadership competencies International Exposure Direct engagement with institutions and communities Understanding of transatlantic challenges and opportunities Exposure to diverse policy environments Professional Networking Connections with accomplished leaders Access to influential decision-makers Long-term professional relationships Community Building Participation in a strong fellowship cohort Opportunities for collaborative problem-solving Lifelong professional friendships Alumni Network Access MMF alumni become part of an extensive international network of leaders who continue to collaborate and support one another. The Impact of the Fellowship Over the past four decades, the Marshall Memorial Fellowship has developed an impressive network of alumni who have gone on to hold influential positions across government, business, civil society, academia, media, and international organizations. The fellowship continues to generate significant impact by: Supporting leadership excellence Strengthening democratic institutions Encouraging innovation in public service Building resilient transatlantic partnerships Promoting constructive dialogue across sectors and borders Alumni Community One of the most valuable aspects of the Marshall Memorial Fellowship is its extensive alumni network. The program has supported cohorts of fellows for decades, including participants from: 2013 2014 2015 2016 2017 2018 2019 2020 2022 2023 2025 This vibrant alumni community continues to contribute to discussions on international cooperation, democracy, public policy, leadership, and social innovation. Funding and Program Support The Marshall Memorial Fellowship is made possible through the generous support of several partners and contributors, including: The German Marshall Fund of the United States The Kresge Foundation The Cleveland Foundation Phokion Potamianos Fellowship alumni contributions Their support enables the program to continue investing in the next generation of transatlantic leaders. Application Process The fellowship s application and selection process generally includes: Submission of an online application. Review of eligibility requirements. Submission of required documents. Evaluation by the selection committee. Interviews for shortlisted candidates. Final selection and notification. Applicants who have applied previously may be eligible to reapply, subject to program rules and requirements. Interested candidates should carefully review official eligibility criteria and application instructions once the 2027 application cycle officially opens. Why You Should Apply The Marshall Memorial Fellowship is an exceptional opportunity for emerging leaders seeking to strengthen their leadership capacity, expand their international network, and contribute meaningfully to transatlantic cooperation. By participating in this highly respected fellowship, fellows gain unique experiences that help them navigate complex global challenges while building lifelong professional relationships. For individuals committed to leadership, democracy, public service, and international collaboration, the Marshall Memorial Fellowship represents a transformative career and personal development opportunity. Deadline Application deadline for the 2027 Marshall Memorial Fellowship: Not yet specified in the provided information. Applicants should monitor the official program website for updates. Official Website Learn more and apply through the official Marshall Memorial Fellowship page: Marshall Memorial Fellowship (GMF) Apply here Learn More For more information about this opportunity, Click here More global Fellowship opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here The post Marshall Memorial Fellowship 2027: Fully Funded Transatlantic Leadership Program for Emerging Leaders appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: DIANA Challenges for 2027: Complete Guide to NATO’s Defence Innovation Accelerator Programme, Funding, Eligibility, and How to Apply
summary: The Defence Innovation Accelerator for the North Atlantic (DIANA) has officially launched its 2027 Challenges , opening a competitive global call for innovators with breakthrough technologies in defence, security, and dual-use applications. The programme is designed to identify, fund, test, and scale emerging technologies that can address urgent and future-facing security needs across NATO and allied nations. Through its structured Accelerator Programme, DIANA provides innovators with funding, expert support, and access to a world-class test centre network. At its core, DIANA seeks to bridge the gap between cutting-edge innovation and real-world defence adoption , ensuring that new technologies move quickly from prototype to operational deployment. What is DIANA? (Defence Innovation Accelerator for the North Atlantic) DIANA is a NATO-aligned innovation initiative that focuses on identifying and accelerating emerging technologies capable of solving critical defence and security challenges. DIANA operates through structured challenge calls, where companies submit proposals addressing specific problem statements. Successful applicants become “DIANA Innovators” and enter a multi-stage acceleration pathway. How the DIANA Programme Works The DIANA innovation pipeline is structured into four main phases: 1. Innovator Selection DIANA launches public challenge calls Companies submit technical proposals Applications are evaluated based on: Alignment with challenge needs Technical feasibility Novelty and innovation Dual-use potential Commercial viability Successful applicants are selected as DIANA Innovators and enter a formal framework agreement. 2. Accelerator Programme Selected innovators enter the DIANA Accelerator Programme, which includes: Contractual funding of €100,000 Technical mentoring and guidance Access to DIANA’s test centre network Opportunity to refine and validate solutions with end users This phase focuses on iterating and improving solutions based on real defence requirements. 3. Down-Selection Process At the end of the accelerator stage: A competitive evaluation takes place Only selected innovators progress further Evaluation criteria include technical maturity, defence relevance, and commercial potential 4. Mission Track Selected participants may enter the Mission Track, which provides: Additional funding of up to €300,000 Advanced demonstration opportunities Investor engagement support Pathways to defence adoption contracts 5. Adoption Pathways All DIANA innovators are supported in identifying pathways for: NATO and allied defence adoption Commercial scaling opportunities Long-term integration into security ecosystems Dynamic Challenges: Fast-Track Innovation Opportunities In addition to annual challenge calls, DIANA also runs Dynamic Challenges throughout the year. These are designed for: Urgent defence needs Rapid technology deployment Highly specific capability gaps Key features include: Rapid iteration and demonstration cycles Prototype contracts Faster adoption pathways Funding support for selected innovators Example highlighted initiative: Decision Superiority for NATO Warfighters Challenge Important distinction: Dynamic Challenge participants typically do not enter the full Accelerator Programme , as these challenges focus on mature solutions ready for rapid deployment. Funding Opportunities in DIANA Challenges Participants in the DIANA ecosystem may receive: €100,000 initial Accelerator funding Up to €300,000 additional Mission Track funding Prototype contracts for selected Dynamic Challenge participants Access to investors and defence procurement pathways This makes DIANA one of the most structured defence innovation funding pipelines in Europe and North America. How to Apply for DIANA Challenges The application process is designed to be competitive and multi-layered. Step 1: Business Identification Applicants must provide: Legal company name Registration details Headquarters location Ownership and control declarations Step 2: Short Form Proposal A concise technical submission focusing on: Core innovation Problem-solving approach Technical feasibility Step 3: Long Form Proposal Expanded submission covering: Dual-use potential Commercial viability Defence and security relevance Market adoption strategy Resource requirements Step 4: Virtual Panel Presentation Shortlisted applicants present to an expert evaluation panel including: Technical specialists Defence experts Commercial advisors Evaluation Criteria DIANA evaluates proposals using strict criteria: Technical Criteria Feasibility Validity Alignment with challenge objectives Innovation level Defence Security Criteria Operational relevance Impact on NATO capabilities Commercial Criteria Market viability Adoption strategy General Criteria Resource availability Programme suitability Who Can Apply? (Eligibility Requirements) DIANA is open to companies that meet specific eligibility conditions: Eligible Applicants: Startups Small and Medium Enterprises (SMEs) Private technology companies Not Eligible: Universities (as primary applicants) Non-profit organizations (direct submission not allowed, but collaboration may occur) Technology Readiness: Minimum TRL 4 and above Early-stage concepts below TRL 4 are not accepted Compliance Requirements: Agreement to competitive selection terms and conditions Valid legal business registration Apply Here Key Benefits of Joining DIANA Challenges Successful applicants gain access to: Direct funding support NATO-aligned defence ecosystem exposure High-level technical validation facilities Rapid testing environments Investment and scaling opportunities Potential defence procurement pathways Important Deadline Submission deadline: 3 July (as specified in the official call) After submission, selected applicants proceed through competitive evaluation stages DIANA Global Presence DIANA operates across multiple international hubs: Headquarters: London, United Kingdom Regional Hub: Tallinn, Estonia North American Office: Halifax, Canada Conclusion The DIANA Challenges for 2027 represent one of the most significant global opportunities for defence and dual-use innovators. With structured funding, technical support, and direct pathways to NATO adoption, the programme is designed to accelerate technologies that can reshape the future of defence and security. Innovators with mature, scalable technologies at TRL 4+ are strongly encouraged to apply before the deadline. For more information, visit the official website Discover more global innovation competition opportunities on OFY, VISIT HERE The post DIANA Challenges for 2027: Complete Guide to NATO’s Defence Innovation Accelerator Programme, Funding, Eligibility, and How to Apply appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .
